slides.title: Why so many men wake up to *pee at night*
slides.sub: The prostate quietly enlarges with age and presses on the urethra. A few habits can ease the flow.

slides.title: An aging prostate squeezes
slides.body: With age the prostate often enlarges, a common change called BPH. It can *press on the urethra* and slow the stream.

slides.title: Weak flow, frequent trips
slides.body: A slower stream, urgency and waking to urinate are typical. These changes *are linked to* an enlarging prostate, not a failing one.

slides.title: Stay lean and active
slides.body: Carrying less belly fat and moving daily *can support* easier flow. Regular activity is linked to fewer urinary symptoms over time.

slides.title: Mind caffeine and fluids
slides.body: Caffeine and alcohol can irritate the bladder. Easing off in the evening *may reduce* nighttime trips and bladder strain.

slides.title: Small habits add up
slides.body: Steady movement, a healthy weight and smarter evening fluids work together. Tracking them makes the *helpful patterns* easy to see.

slides.title: Track the habits that *ease flow*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your movement, steps, food and sleep, then turns them into one daily Health Score so the habits that help stay front of mind.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
If you are up at night more than you used to be, the aging prostate is often the quiet reason. 🌿

Staying lean, moving daily and easing off evening caffeine can support easier flow and fewer nighttime trips. Save this for later. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
